question: Explain how virtualization technology contributes to sustainable cloud computing practices, and provide two specific examples of its environmental benefits.
expectedPoints: Virtualization allows multiple virtual machines to run on a single physical server, improving hardware utilization rates
Reduces total number of physical servers required, leading to lower energy consumption
Decreases electronic waste by extending hardware lifespan through better resource allocation

question: Compare and contrast the sustainability impacts of traditional data centers versus modern cloud data centers, focusing on at least three key technological differences.
expectedPoints: Modern cloud data centers utilize advanced cooling technologies (e.g., liquid cooling, free cooling) that are more energy-efficient than traditional air conditioning systems
Cloud providers implement sophisticated power management systems with dynamic resource allocation that minimizes idle server energy consumption
Large cloud data centers can achieve better Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) ratios through scale economies and specialized infrastructure design

question: Describe how artificial intelligence and machine learning are being used to optimize energy efficiency in cloud computing environments.
expectedPoints: AI-powered workload forecasting predicts demand patterns to optimize server provisioning and reduce idle capacity
Machine learning algorithms optimize cooling systems by analyzing real-time temperature data and adjusting cooling resources dynamically
Intelligent workload placement algorithms distribute computing tasks to locations and times where renewable energy is most available
